findings.title: The grid names its own choke point
findings.stat: A row literally named LEYTE_TO_CEBU appears in the day-ahead runs on 75 of 99 days
findings.blurb: IEMOP's congestions-manifesting files put named equipment at 100% of its binding limit. The Tabango (Leyte) to Daanbantayan (Cebu) 230 kV lines that carry this corridor top the league: at a limit on 96 of 99 archive days across the day-ahead and real-time runs, including 138 5-minute real-time intervals on 26 days. The same corridor IEMOP's December 2025 report names in prose.
findings.source: IEMOP congestions-manifesting files (RTD + DAP), archive window

findings.title: The announced wave is the size of the margin
findings.stat: 1,500 MW forecast (DICT, by 2028) against a 3,629 MW May 2026 system supply margin
findings.blurb: The DICT forecast alone equals 41% of the whole system's May margin; Meralco has committed 1,000 MW for 10 data centers. The 11 pinned sites with a public figure name 591.3 MW between them, and a data center is near-flat 24/7 load: it consumes margin in every interval, not just at the evening peak.
findings.source: IEMOP May 2026 report; DICT via BusinessWorld; PCIJ

findings.title: Reserves ran below requirement on most days
findings.stat: Luzon scheduled reserves fell below the stated requirement on 62 of 99 archive days
findings.blurb: In the operator's own dispatch schedules, load was curtailed on 101 grid-days in this window (5,191.1 MWh), and the Visayas ran short of scheduled reserves on 64 days. Thin margins are the observed normal state, not a forecast.
findings.source: IEMOP RTD regional summaries, archive window

findings.title: The market prices the geography the day it comes back
findings.stat: Administered window: the three grids priced within P0.015/kWh of each other. Market window: a mean daily high-to-low spread of P5.88/kWh across the three grids
findings.blurb: While WESM was suspended (through 2026-05-01), daily regional prices were near-identical. After trading resumed, the islands split: Luzon P7.65, Visayas P12.96, Mindanao P11.52 per kWh on average, with 28 days spreading beyond P5/kWh and a widest daily spread of P15.722/kWh on 2026-06-08. The links between the islands are the reason the numbers differ.
findings.source: IEMOP load-weighted average prices (final), archive window

findings.title: One plant trip takes 18% of the margin with it
findings.stat: One 647 MW Sual unit equals 18% of the May 2026 system margin
findings.blurb: Sual's two 647 MW units are the largest single contingencies on the Luzon grid. The archive's outage schedules list a Sual unit out on 0 day(s) in this window; the map's toggle subtracts one unit from the published margin as arithmetic, not a dispatch simulation.
findings.source: IEMOP outage schedules used in RTD; IEMOP May 2026 report

findings.title: A 52-day Visayas alert streak just ended
findings.blurb: The streak ended when one 150 MW unit returned; 935.3 MW was still unavailable that day. In the same window the dispatch schedules show the Visayas short of scheduled reserves on 64 days, with 514.61 MWh of load curtailed in the schedules on 2026-06-25 alone.
findings.source: NGCP advisories via SunStar and GMA (Jul 1-2, 2026); IEMOP RTD regional summaries
